name: Honoka Kosaka
personality: Honoka is an optimist who is never afraid to take risks. She is always in a good mood and likes to look at the bright side of things. She is determined and once her mind is set on something, nothing can stop her. This is believed to be the driving force behind μ's formation and success. But because of her persistent personality, she often overexerts herself.Her personality is similar to Rin Hoshizora's because they are both determined, hard-working, and fun-loving.She likes to eat bread, but she doesn't like eating red bean paste or white bean paste despite working at her family's Japanese sweets shop.

name: Eli Ayase
personality: Eli is shown to be a perfect role model to the students in Otonokizaka High School. As the former student council president, she is often portrayed as a cold and strict character due to her responsibilities and being against the idea of Honoka Kosaka and her friends forming an idol group to get more attention from the public. However, ironically, Nozomi Tojo reveals that Eli was also trying to save the school but was denied permission to take action by the chairwoman.Eli is highly respected and well-known among the students, especially among her juniors, which call her 'Eli-senpai'. Unlike Nozomi, she appears rather stoic and skeptical in the early episodes, but it is shown that Eli is actually very kind and caring towards people who are close to her, like her little sister. She is also dependable as her grandmother entrusted her the responsibility of preventing the school from being shut down. Other than being admirable, Eli also has a lot of fans in the school. After she joined the group, their popularity rose and their ranking among the school idols increased, achieving more fame.Honoka also complimented her beauty, saying that she is beautiful, tall, and has a great body shape. On top of that, she is also very mature and level-headed and acts as a strategist in promoting their group, as she planned to introduce μ's in Akihabara by having a street performance.Despite being portrayed as level-headed, Eli can sometimes have an unexpected side to her, and it was revealed by Nozomi that she almost ate a plastic chocolate once, thinking it was real.

name: Kotori Minami
personality: Kotori appears to be a feminine, caring, shy, and cheerful girl. She is known for having her head in the clouds. Despite this, she is μ's designer alongside Nico Yazawa, and is also clever and good at studying, being a honor student. Kotori has a very huge aptitude for fashion and is very good at drawing costumes for the group. Kotori is shown to be very kind and supportive to her friends and genuinely cares about them, especially their feelings: she's easily the most altruistic character in her group, and cares more how others feel than how she does herself. However, this can go too far as Kotori usually backs Honoka up when she (Honoka) is scolded by Umi.Kotori is also shown as the most indecisive person among all nine members and tends to have Umi Sonoda and Honoka Kosaka, her childhood friends, make decisions for her. She also has a hard time expressing her feelings, since she always takes other people's feelings into consideration. Kotori is a kind person, but she can also be gullible.In Kotori's Holiday (July 2012), it is revealed that while naive, Kotori is hard-working, and the type of person who can't relax unless all the work is done as soon as possible.

name: Umi Sonoda
personality: Umi has the qualities of a yamato nadeshiko, or a proper and traditional Japanese lady. As such, she is generally shy when having to interact with unfamiliar people. Even with those she is comfortable with, she seems to act reserved by default unless provoked. She is highly intelligent and a very logical solution-seeker. Above all, she is extremely disciplined--nearly to a fault-- and as a result can often be hard on others. However, this is never motivated by feelings of malice or dislike aimed at her friends. Umi is often overly strict towards her friends, but is the most unforgiving with/hardest on herself. She despises laziness, shortcuts, and/or cheating; she instead places a lot of value in hard work and perseverance.As self-destructive as this may be, these qualities combined with Umi's strong will makes her well-suited to leadership, which she often utilizes by being one of the main organizers of logistical or business-related action items for µ's. Where her leadership skills lack in sociability, she makes up in organizational excellence. This, in tandem with Umi's inclination to stay in the background when she can, makes Umi the perfect second-in-command to Honoka's leadership in µ's. In this manner, the two operate perfectly as the primary leaders in µ's. Honoka establishes and maintains µ's core of friendship and love for both members and fans alike through her ability to unify, inspire, and motivate. Umi facilitates the less enjoyable, yet arguably more vital, aspects of their idol work.The foil between Honoka and Umi's personalities affect areas outside of leading µ's. Honoka is more of a happy-go-lucky, modern day schoolgirl, which lead to her impulsive goal of becoming a school idol. Due to Umi's traditional values of proper ladylike behavior, Umi resisted Honoka's pleas for Umi to join her. Umi's strict moral compass made nearly all aspects of being an idol highly inappropriate and embarrassing for her. However, she is shown at times to entertain intrusive thoughts in which she acts entirely contrary to her code of proper behavior. One example of this is when in the middle of practicing archery, she imagines herself as a cheery and energetic idol saying (what will later become) her signature catchphrase Love arrow, shoot! (ラブアローシュート); Umi is greatly flustered and ashamed of even having such a thought. When she does eventually agree to becoming a school idol, she is at first uncomfortable with wearing short skirts and opts for pant shorts. However, she learns to get used to skirts as time goes on.Despite her overall calm and collected personality, there are a few exceptions. The rest of µ's learns (the hard way) that Umi gets very grumpy and angry if she is woken up in the middle of sleeping.  Umi's moral aversion to dishonesty is also actually just a literal inability to lie: when playing Old Maid, Umi is painfully unable to keep a poker face. Any emotion she feels is immediately obvious by her facial expressions. In this way, Umi continually loses every game, which frustrates her, because she is entirely unaware of this behavior. Her persistence and determination causes her to repeatedly ask to play another game, but her unawareness of her emotive reactions does not relent.Ultimately, although Umi is rather strict, especially towards Honoka, she is a genuinely kind person and a very loyal friend who does the best she can to help her friends. She's also well-mannered and always speaks politely, using です (desu), ます (masu), and other polite forms of speaking in her speech.

name: Rin Hoshizora
personality: Rin appears to be a bit of a tomboy, though with a girly streak. She likes sports and is arguably the most athletic member of μ's, on par with Eli. Like Honoka, Rin is very energetic, hard-working and active. One key difference between her and Honoka, however, is that Rin is known for being very mindful of other people's opinions, as she puts their feelings first before taking action into something. This is shown multiple times throughout the anime.Part of Rin's tomboyish personality stems from the fact she was teased by her classmates for wearing skirts and dresses in public, to the point she stopped wearing them as often, and wore shorts or pants instead. This insecurity later grew into a complex as time passed, to the point she no longer wore skirts or dresses, other than for her uniform or her idol outfits. This is also further compounded by her petite body shape. With the help of her friends, Rin is able to get over this complex due to being the center of a live performance where she has to wear a dress, and she is fully able of embracing her feminine side, wearing skirts once again.Rin has a very mischievous side, and likes to playfully tease her best friend Hanayo. She also likes hugs, and has attempted to hug every member of μ's. She enjoys acting like a cat, complete with a curved mouth reminiscent of one, which also translates to her energetic and mischievous self.

name: Maki Nishikino
personality: Maki has the qualities of a tsundere, and she has a hard time admitting her true feelings to her peers. She also has somewhat childish beliefs which are undoubtedly reflections on her somewhat sheltered upbringing. One such example is her uninterrupted belief in Santa Claus, which the rest of the group finds out about while together. Maki continues on to describe (somewhat proudly) how she cleans the chimney for Santa, and how he never misses her house. Nico attempts to disillusion her, but is held back by other members who did not want to ruin Maki's belief.Maki was initially portrayed as a loner in the first few episodes, and spent her time alone in the music room. Her standoffishness can be attributed to her tsundere qualities in which although she may want to get close to others, she cannot admit that and refuses, as best she can, to show it. Throughout the series, Maki opens up more and more, but elements of her solitary life before µ's crop up from time to time with her awkwardness and inability to properly admit her investment in their friendship.Maki is also quite stubborn. This was shown when she was invited to play at the beach, as she was withdrawn from the group and not interested in playing with them.Although Maki is not conceited nor stuck up, there are moments where her pride combined with her privileged and sheltered upbringing with few other friends lead Maki to unintentionally act insensitively or arrogantly. Despite this, µ's understands that Maki has no such intentions, and usually any questionable interaction with Maki is usually because of her tsundere side preventing her from speaking how she really feels.Her personality often clashes with Nico Yazawa. Rin tells her 'You're one to talk', implying that Maki is stubborn as well.

name: Nozomi Tojo
personality: As the former vice-president of the student council and the eldest member of μ's, Nozomi appears as a pretty wise, mysterious and mature girl with an almost all-knowing vibe to her, due to her tarot reading and fortune-telling skills. She's gentle and kind, as well as rather conspirative, usually being the first person to advise μ's whenever they run into an obstacle or trouble, and will usually remain calm and collected during most situations. This carries on even after joining μ's.Nozomi has a very mischievous side, often teasing the other girls and causing them to get flustered. At times, she can seem perverted as well, as she sometimes threatens to punish some of the members of μ's by grabbing their breasts from behind, her most common target being Nico. This punishment was deemed the washi washi by Nozomi herself.While mischievous, Nozomi also cares deeply for the other girls, and she highly cherishes the friendships the group has formed between them. Since Nozomi and her family had moved around for most of her childhood, she was very shy and never really had any friends. She began to break out of her shell after meeting Eli, even though she is still unable to bring herself to approach anybody else until Honoka decided to form μ's. Nozomi states she had always wanted to do something with her friends, and considers the nine members' friendships with the other a miracle.

name: Hanayo Koizumi
personality: Hanayo is a very shy, quiet character who doesn't normally voice her opinions. She is very reserved and will often let other people make decisions for her because of this. Hanayo is also easily scared. However, she has a very passionate side, and her behavior changes when it comes to school idols and anything related, as she is very enthusiastic about them. She doesn't usually state her desires either, so when she wanted to become an idol she didn't say so for a while and needed a push from both Maki and Rin to become one. Despite her timid nature, she is genuinely sweet and kind-hearted, and is considerate towards her friends and their feelings, similar to Kotori Minami.Hanayo loves to eat anything, especially rice balls. For that reason, she usually gets upset with her weight.

name: Nico Yazawa
personality: Normally, Nico has quite an abrasive personality, but when she takes on her alternate personality, she becomes a happy, upbeat character who is always smiling, often saying her catchphrase, 'Nico Nico Nii!' (にっこにっこにー), accompanied with a pose. She usually thinks a bit highly of herself but, despite that, she is also caring.It is later revealed that her obsession with performative, over-the-top happiness and cuteness is because of the difficulty of her home situation. Her family circumstances and the role she plays in it have led her to fixate on both making sure as many people as possible are smiling and also seeking and receiving attention and praise. This manifests in her idol obsession and subsequent persona.A common joke in the series is that she's often ignored by the other members of μ's when she claims that she is the better idol. Sometimes, her determination to become the best idol causes her to be a little bit cold, calculating, and somewhat ill-intentioned.Nico often gets annoyed with the former student council president, Eli Ayase, for taking away the spotlight from her with Eli's beauty and maturity.She also argues a lot with Maki, since their personalities often clash together. However, they have a good friendship and they actually care about each other.

name: Chika Takami
personality: Chika is very energetic, goofy, sociable and has a very strong sense of perseverance. Her positive, can-do attitude gradually begins to affect everyone around her as the anime progresses. Her energetic and perseverant nature also makes her rather impatient and easy to tease about giving up, only to make her even more perseverant on achieving her goal. Due to her usual positive nature, however, she tends to hide her more sensitive side and tries hard not to cry when she is sad or frustrated, as she feels it would drag down others' morale if they were to see her feeling sad. Chika heavily dislikes when her friends get into confrontations, she deems petty, being one of the very few things that can anger her. She mediates disputes among her friends when tensions arise. She loves and treasures the friendships she's made throughout her years at school, especially with You, who has been her childhood friend. Since Riko moved to Uchiura, Chika becomes very interested in befriending her new fellow second year. She's not above making snide, deadpan expressions whenever one of her friends is acting strange, such as with Yoshiko. In one of the audio dramas, it is revealed Chika not only has a habit of making puns, but of explaining them without being asked as well.

name: Riko Sakurauchi
personality: Riko is a reserved, kind, polite and modest girl who likes indoor activities, acting as a foil to her friends, the more energetic Chika and You. She is rather shy, but is nevertheless affable and friendly. However, while she is generally quite levelheaded, calm and mature, she often flies into a panic when under pressure, jumping to conclusions and making mistakes. Because of this, and a previous incident on which she failed to play the piano in front of a live audience, she doesn't think she's special, and doesn't want to become a school idol. However, her friend Chika drags her into it, in the hopes this would inspire her to keep playing the piano. In spite of her quiet and reserved personality, Riko has cynophobia: she loses her calm when she sees a dog (or anything she deems similar to one), to the point of moving recklessly when faced with one directly. After she and Yoshiko Tsushima temporarily take care of a dog, her fear of dogs is revoked and she eventually understands she likes them, getting her own. Riko can also be quite playful and dorky, this is especially seen with Chika. Additionally, her interactions with Yoshiko cause Riko to start acting like her at times and be called 'Lili' (Riri) by her, which annoys Riko. (This name is derived from Lilith, a demon in Jewish mythology, and is also the double pronunciation of the first syllable of her name.) Riko is also a fan of certain types of girls' love doujinshi, and is very timid about this. When her friends see her with said doujinshi, she panics and acts defensively by hiding them, saying they were only gifts.

name: You Watanabe
personality: You is an energetic, tomboyish and positive girl with an outlook of focusing on her goals, which can sometimes cause her to take action without thinking things over first. Her positive attitude also makes her one of the more charismatic members of the group. Since she would like to have her father's job as the captain of a ferry, she tends to salute when she says her catchphrase 'Yousoro!', often in the place of 'hello' and 'good-bye'. You loves the friendship she has forged with Chika over the years, so she slowly grew jealous of Riko Sakurauchi's friendship with Chika when she invites her to join Aqours. After Mari Ohara confronts her on it, You expresses her feelings to her best friend, and stops being jealous of Riko.

name: Yoshiko Tsushima
personality: Yoshiko seems to be a chuunibyou, a person who holds delusions of grandeur. Bright, self-assured, smart, and thoughtful, Yoshiko has a persona who she describes as 'Yohane', a 'fallen angel' who has angered God and was cast from the heavens to Earth. Apparently, a side effect to being a 'fallen angel' is that she is cursed with extremely bad luck, running into all kinds of unforeseen trouble wherever she goes: raining whenever she goes on trips, falling whenever it snows, not winning anything from lucky draws in convenience stores, falling sick right before exams, and so on. Because of this, she often blames herself for Aqours' misfortunes. Through her delusional persona, Yoshiko acts dramatic, cunning, and a little bit devious, and often goes as far as using dark, devilish accessories or clothing to further accentuate her 'fallen angel' look as 'Yohane'. When acting like her, Yoshiko's voice is significantly lower-pitched. Yoshiko has embraced this side of herself so much, she hates to be called by her real name and demands to be referred to as 'Yohane' instead, going as far as to cross out her real name in her introduction and replace it as such as well as claiming herself to be the goddaughter of 'Satan' Lucifer. As a result of embracing her 'Yohane' side, it is hard for Yoshiko to act like a normal person (something she herself is aware of). This causes her difficulty to get along properly with her classmates and, at times, the rest of Aqours. She is often teased by her friends Ruby and Hanamaru for acting like 'Yohane', which is annoying for her. Such moments can cause her voice to reveal her actual, high-pitched, voice. In spite of all this, Yoshiko is a genuinely kind and decent girl, and isn't ill-willed by nature; she does treasure her friendship with the rest of Aqours for accepting her 'Yohane' side; this is most evident with Ruby and Hanamaru. Additionally, she affectionately refers to her friends as 'little demons' (リトルデーモン Ritoru dēmon), particularly Riko Sakurauchi, whom she refers to as 'Little Demon Lili/Riri'. Her kindness is also shown when she rescues a dog along with Riko, and helps her friend overcome her fear of dogs. Despite her 'Yohane' side, Yoshiko is also able to pull off a normal conversation when it's really needed. Due to the fact the second-years are considered 'the heart' of Aqours, Yoshiko has some difficulty relating to the third-years, which is shared with Hanamaru; this is due to her preference for the indoors and for calmer activities. Yoshiko likes extremely spicy food, as she ate takoyaki filled with Tabasco sauce. She also dislikes coffee.

name: Hanamaru Kunikida
personality: Hanamaru is a hardworking girl who diligently works at her family's temple. She spends a lot of time reading alone and is easily distracted by books. Since she was raised as a temple girl, Hanamaru does not watch television much, nor does she use the internet, making her outdated. Because of this, she is fascinated with modern technology, yelling her catchphrase 'Mirai zura!' (未来ずら 'It's the future, zura!') when she sees something advanced such as a computer. She was often teased due to this, but was usually defended by her best friend Ruby; however, the first time it happened, Ruby ended up crying, causing Hanamaru to comfort her instead. Hanamaru seems to be quite self-depreciating, calling herself a 'chibi procrastinator' and 'neither cute nor interesting'. She aspires to be like Ruby, whom she sees as 'beautiful, cute and dazzling'. She is also gentle and caring for those around her. Despite her appearance, Hanamaru is also smart and not easily fooled. Due to the fact the Second Years are considered 'the heart' of Aqours, Hanamaru has some difficulty relating to the Third Years, which is shared with Yoshiko; this is due to her preference for the indoors and for calmer activities. Hanamaru is shown to have a playful and mischievous side; this is especially present when she teases Yoshiko's Fallen Angel behavior and her embarrassing moments (often alongside Ruby), which annoys Yoshiko all the time. She has a surprisingly large appetite, able to eat a helping of food quickly and often asking for another one immediately after, and is usually seen eating sweets in the background, the most common of them being a roll of noppo bread. Hanamaru stated that since she was little, she was a quiet girl who played off to the side.

name: Ruby Kurosawa
personality: Ruby is very shy and timid, which makes it hard for her to get along with other people. She has a tendency to utter or scream 'Pigii!!!' when she doesn't like something; in the event she screams, her face will turn red as she does so. She has never talked to any males besides her father before, so she has always been bad at dealing with the male gender. Although she is fearful and easily upset, she does have a tough interior forged by her role as the daughter of a rich, well-known family. Ruby is very close to her friend, Hanamaru. From a different perspective, Ruby is adaptable and mostly admired by her friends, and they support her no matter what; despite the fact the Second Years are considered 'the heart' of Aqours, Ruby does not have difficulty getting along with the rest of the group unlike her fellow First Years or the Third Years. Her catchphrase is 'Ganbarubii!' (がんばルビィ！ lit. 'Rubesty'), a portmanteau between 'ganbaru' (頑張る try with full effort) and her own name; she uses this phrase to encourage herself or others to do their very best. Ruby also speaks in the third person from time to time, just like Hanamaru. However, she tends to use 'watashi' as a first-person pronoun. Due to the strong bond she shares with her sister Dia, Ruby will immediately display her tough interior and defend her sister when a negative point of her is brought up. Ruby is also shown to have a playful and mischievous side when she teases Yoshiko Tsushima's Fallen Angel behavior and her embarrassing moments (alongside Hanamaru), which annoys Yoshiko all the time. She also despises spicy food.

name: Kanan Matsuura
personality: Breezily mature, Kanan rarely sweats the details and usually maintains a calm, cool and even stoic demeanor. Though she may seem a bit bland at first, she loves sports and nature, claiming that she is happy every day just by being able to see the dazzling sun, the blue skies and the sea. She isn't as talkative as her fellow third years, speaking the least of them in the anime, but is nevertheless friendly and laid-back. Kanan is a very kind, warm-hearted and altruistic individual. She deeply cares for her friends, being very supportive and often putting their needs above her own, going to great lengths to ensure they get what she thinks is best for them. However, due to this, Kanan can fail to realize the others' feelings the same way she does, and she will go to the same lengths to hide the truth if it will hurt the person. This particularly caused her friend, Mari, to accept the chance of studying abroad when in reality she didn't want to, as Mari was content with being with her friends. After Mari goes to study abroad, this causes Kanan to live with the weight of her lie, acting much more distant and aloof to Mari when she returns. When Mari learns the truth, confronting Kanan and expressing how much she cares for her, Kanan finally reconciles with her old friend, stops being distant, and becomes friendly once again. Due to the fact the second years are considered 'the heart' of Aqours, Kanan has some difficulty relating to the first years, which is shared with Mari; this is due to her preference for the outdoors and for sports. While athletic, mature and composed, Kanan has a sweet and soft personality. She is also afraid of strange, creaking sounds and afraid of heights. She also likes hugs ever since her childhood and will often hug her friends if given the opportunity, usually declaring her catchphrase 'Hagu shiyo' (ハグしよ 'Let's hug') before so. Although Kanan is intelligent and level-headed, she doesn't get very good grades. This is due to her poor attendance at school due to the fact she must help her family with work; with that said, it's also seen she doesn't like reading and easily gets tired of it.

name: Dia Kurosawa
personality: Dia is a very refined, polite and mature young woman who speaks in a formal style of Japanese, regarding all of her friends with politeness by referring to each of them as '-san', and usually ending her phrases with '-desu wa.' Strict, hardworking, and a bit of a perfectionist, she dislikes it when things are done sloppily or incorrectly, which is part of the reason why she initially rejected Chika's application for a school idol club. She is very meticulous regarding small details, as far as going on tirades regarding the unorthodox reading of certain Kanji. Though always trying to remain dignified and serious, Dia has a short temper, and she can be quite brash and melodramatic, while she also has a smiley heart and is very kind to her group. Whenever she denies something, she uses her catchphrase 'Buu buu desu wa!' (ブッブーですわ！ 'Bzzt bzzt, it is not!'), as if imitating a buzzer. She also tends to become overly zealous when school idols are involved, as she is very passionate about the subject. Because Dia can be quite harsh, she initially had the most difficulty in getting along with the rest of Aqours with the exception of her fellow third years, despite being adaptable like Ruby; this is especially notable when referring to the second years. Although she is formal and polite, she also wishes the rest of the group referred to her as a friend by using more informal pronouns much like the other third years, despite referring to them with politeness. She eventually becomes more patient towards them, and the group refers to her informally more often, displaying more closeness to them. Dia can be described as a tsundere. While she is strict with a tough exterior, Dia is genuinely kind deep down and cares for all of her friends, particularly her younger sister Ruby, whom she is very protective and nurturing of. She wants nothing less than total success for Aqours, and constantly pushes herself and her friends to be the best they can possibly be in order to achieve their goals. According to Mari and Kanan, Dia has a habit of scratching her beauty mark whenever she is telling a lie. Flashbacks of her younger self reveal she used to be much like her sister Ruby, fairly timid, fearful and easily moved to tears, though she did start to develop her more serious side as time passed.

name: Mari Ohara
personality: Mari has a cheerful, eccentric and mischievous personality, makes many jokes, and often prefers to work by herself. She always keeps her chin up when faced with hardship so as not to burden others, and is ready to try her hand at anything. However, as a half-foreigner, she does not feel that she is very suited to being a Japanese-style idol. Despite Mari's cheerful personality, there are times when she can be serious. Mari speaks with an accent, and she has a high, cheerful voice. Mari often inserts English words (Italian in the English dub) into her sentences, but when she is sad or serious, the pitch of her voice becomes lower, she loses her accent, and she uses English words less.

name: Yu Takasaki
personality: Yu is insightful, reliable, quick to take action, and very caring of the school idols. She is shown to have a deep passion for school idols, which inspires her goals and actions. She is very kind, empathetic and sociable, easily creating bonds with others. She has a passionate and charismatic nature, which easily attracts others. However, her passion for school idols and putting her efforts in sometimes makes her neglect other responsibilities.

name: Shizuku Osaka
personality: Shizuku is a sweet, polite, level-headed and mature honor student. She is considered a traditional Japanese beauty and is quite timid at times. Despite her somewhat shy nature, she is friendly and generally cheerful. She is genuinely selfless and generous, offering her home for others to study together and helping those with difficulty. Her responsible nature inclines her to take on too much responsibility and keep her thoughts to herself. She worries about things quite often. Shizuku is passionate about acting and is very skilled at getting into character. She has a love of old movies and novels that has made her anxious because she felt she was the only child who liked these things, afraid to be seen as an oddball by her peers. Despite her somewhat stiff demeanor and lack of self-expression, Shizuku is a good person who genuinely wants to help others, and often yearns to connect with others deep down.

name: Kasumi Nakasu
personality: Although slightly self-centered and mischievous, Kasumi is a kind, friendly and lively girl who loves all things cute, and her love for school idols is unrivaled in strength and inspiration. Kasumi wants to be number one in any competition she is in and often resorts to cheating or sabotage to achieve that goal. As a consequence, she is a sore loser whenever she achieves anything other than first place. In general, Kasumi spends a lot of her time scheming or thinking up new ways to ensure her own victory; however, these plans often fail or are not as devious as she thinks. Even with her scheming streak, Kasumi has the capacity to be considerate and kind to her fellow school idols. She hates the nickname Kasukasu which roughly translates to "just barely" or "not quite", and gets in a bad mood when she is referred to with it.

name: Rina Tennoji
personality: Rina is not good at conveying emotions on her face. Because of this, she makes use of the "Rina-chan Board", a sketchbook that she draws her emotions on. Rina is still trying to change herself by communicating and talking more with her friends. She admires school idols for their confidence and friendliness. Rina gets nervous easily but continues to try her best.

name: Shioriko Mifune
personality: Shioriko is described as calm and academically gifted. She values Japanese tradition and has been trained in traditional dance and flower arranging. She has a talent for identifying people's strengths and weaknesses, and uses this to help students at Nijigasaki High School succeed in their club activities by offering advice. In the game, she can be overly pragmatic, strict, cold and even antagonistic in her behavior; although people are initially upset upon hearing her suggestions, they become thankful when it leads them to succeeding. Despite her somewhat stiff demeanor and lack of self-expression, Shioriko is a good person who genuinely wants to help others, and often yearns to connect with others deep down.

name: Ai Miyashita
personality: Ai is a suggestible, friendly and sociable person who is always willing to help. She has a lot of friends and can be seen chatting with strangers at any given time. She is very caring, especially with her family and friends. Ai is very empathetic, selfless and supportive, as she loves helping others and is very open about how much she cares about their feelings. She tends to speak very casually, using a lot of slang. She easily laughs at bad puns, too, as she has a rather childish sense of humor. Despite having a flawless image in school and being very popular, Ai is very down-to-earth and does not perceive herself as better than others. She even seems unaware of her reputation, as she never pretends to be someone she is not, nor is she conscious of whatever image she is giving off.

name: Ayumu Uehara
personality: Ayumu is a hardworking girl that puts everything into what she does. She does not believe she is very skilled at her school idol activities, but she continues to try her hardest to excel in them. She is generally insecure in her abilities. Her struggles revolve largely on confidence and how that relates to her pathway to her dream as a school idol. She is kind, cheerful, and friendly, but a bit shy and tends to be clumsy at times. She often responds to mischief in a pure manner, which throws those involved off-guard. She is very protective of her childhood best friend's relationship and is jealous when interactions or praises are directed toward them. In instances where her personality is satirized, she tends to act extremely controlling, pulling her friend off to the side and isolating them from other girls in the club.

name: Setsuna Yuki
personality: Setsuna has an energetic smile and a distinctive style of performing. She is very friendly, charismatic and naturally people-oriented. She is easily excitable and full of energy, and can be moved emotionally very easily. She lights up whenever she is asked a question about her interests, which include anime, manga, video games, and school idols. While she is a very passionate geek and loves to talk about her otaku hobbies with her friends, due to her status as a serious and hard-working student council president, she feels she has to hide them from her mother. She takes her passions very seriously and always wants to improve. She wants to share her love of school idols with the world, and help others feel more comfortable saying what they love, but she can be too passionate about idol activities. She tries to encourage everyone to pursue their dreams and is open to helping others with their dancing and singing.

name: Kanata Konoe
personality: Kanata is often described as a nemuidere, a person often seen as being very sleepy and frequently takes naps during the day. She is very easygoing and prone to napping in the laps of others, although she seems to like some laps most. She carries a pillow around with her at school. At times, she will appear to be sleeping but is actually awake and listening to what is going on around her. She usually refers to herself in third person when speaking, addressing herself as "Kanata-chan". Despite seeming always tired, Kanata is motivated as a school idol by her younger sister and others' praise. She often mentions her dream of being able to take a nap with the audience during a performance. Kanata loves being doted on, but is also very sisterly and capable of looking after others. She loves being a big sister, and tends to look after the other idols in various, little ways. Kanata can be very diligent in matters that are important to her. Her grades are good despite her sleepiness.

name: Emma Verde
personality: Emma is a kind and easygoing girl with a healing nature. She is very cheerful, pleasant, and tries to look after the other idols. She aims to be encouraging and to help others smile. Like Shizuku, Emma tends to keep her worries to herself because she does not want others to worry about her. She gets nervous easily. Having eight siblings back home, she can relate to Kanata on the joys of being an older sister. Her family regularly sends her video letters so they can stay connected and on weekends, Emma will typically spend an hour chatting with them via webcam. Her dream to become a school idol was the reason why she studied Japanese and eventually came to Japan.

name: Karin Asaka
personality: Karin is very mature and gives off a rather seductive vibe. She uses her adult-like looks to her advantage. She also teaches others how to be sexy and cool like her. However, these lessons sometimes backfire when people begin to show her significant attention or respond in a pure manner, as Karin will become embarrassed. Despite her teasing attitude, she is rather innocent and has a somewhat naive side to her personality. In the anime, her personality is different, being noticeably more reserved and shy. She states she does not like being around many people, and used to be a little aloof at the beginning, but nevertheless is genuinely kind and friendly. She is rather self-conscious about her weaknesses and initially being a loner, and can be naive and clumsy. While quite confident and likes to compete, she is also a genuinely supportive and empathetic person. Because of her modeling activities taking up most of her time, her grades are below average and she has difficulties waking up in the morning.

name: Lanzhu Zhong
personality: Since she was a kid, Lanzhu was treated as a gifted girl due to her noticeable talents in many fields. However, this generated insecurity in her as she was always eventually left by others because she was treated as "special" and not as an equal among her peers. Despite showing herself as a charismatic girl, her unique personality tends to catch others off guard by how demanding she can be with them so that they achieve excellent results like her, which leads her to make questionable decisions and to treat people in a way that usually makes them feel insulted. Even so, she does not hesitate to seek every possible way to befriend others. Lanzhu shows herself to be very competitive and somewhat arrogant, though she does not appear as hostile and rude.

name: Mia Taylor
personality: Mia is a very straightforward and competitive girl, to the point of declaring war on others as a songwriter. However, her confidence is a facade and she actually tends to feel insecure about her songs' quality compared to others. At some point, she tried to determine why she felt this way despite being convinced that her own songs were better than others' and went to discuss the other girl's songwriting skills with Rina, who suggested she get closer to the other girl in order to better understand her.

name: Kanon Shibuya
personality: Kanon is a first-year student and is seen as being somewhat shy and fluttered, admitting that she is not good at communicating in front of others. However, while walking in town, Kanon would often listen to music on her headphones, and would usually find herself singing the words to a song. Although she yearns to sing in front of people, Kanon is particularly nervous about doing so. Despite seeing herself as being very ordinary with no particular talents or aspirations for her life, Kanon wishes to cheer people up through her favorite songs. Kanon is naturally charismatic and people-oriented. She is very kind-hearted, friendly, cheerful, passionate and supportive. She is a selfless and loyal friend, with a sense of justice, and is used to confronting bullies. She is also shown to like animals, as seen when she plays with a stray cat on the street. She has a more boyish and blunt side, displayed when she is at home and in her more nervous moments.

name: Keke Tang
personality: A fun-loving and energetic optimist. Keke holds that the most important things in life are passion and a smiling face. To her, anything is possible if one never gives up, such as being a school idol. When dealing with tense or stressful situations, Keke often recalls the pithy advice her mother once gave: if her emotions were to ever run hot, it is best to 'cool down' with sweets. Her mother's words being the likely inspiration for her sweet tooth, Keke believes that a scoop of ice cream can do more to wash away grouchy feelings than anything else, and could even bring about reconciliation among bickering friends. Her favorite saying is, 'There is no time like the present.'

name: Chisato Arashi
personality: Chisato is very kind, sweet, loyal and supportive towards her friends. Although she describes herself as having a friendly and congenial personality, Chisato admits that others would find her 'scary' whenever she gets angry. She loves to converse with her friends and go shopping together. Chisato is also very well-attuned to the latest news and rumors traveling within her school, seemingly to the extent that she might one day become Yuigaoka's official gossip girl. Chisato likes anything that is round shaped; especially Kanon's pet Mammaru, who seems to be wary around Chisato when she comes to visit.

name: Sumire Heanna
personality: Being in the entertainment world for so long, Sumire believes her experience and knowledge of its workings is second to none. Somewhat of a prima donna, Sumire assumes that most people already know her name. Somehow attached to the idea of her own stardom, Sumire dreams of becoming a famous school idol not just in Japan, but 'across the galaxy.' But Sumire also takes her more earthbound duty as a shrine maiden very seriously, warning those to not aimlessly ask the gods to do everything for them, such as helping them become school idols. Instead, Sumire believes that they should do their best together without help from the gods and make their own wishes come true. Sumire was shown to be a confident character who was very self-assured. However, this was most likely a mask. Sumire always being chosen as second place affects her deeply. Her goal is to become world famous or something similar. However, she feels like she is always the second option. She feels like she is the worst, so she always acts like she is the best. Her favorite saying is, 'Faith will move mountains,' and her catchphrase is 'Galaxy-!!!'

name: Ren Hazuki
personality: Ren is polite and speaks with a very formal tone, although she is not quite used to handling casual conversations. Having been told as a child to refrain from spending too much time online, she also has difficulty communicating on camera. But considering that Ren is a fairly disciplined individual who manages her time well, she could easily dispense with any form of small talk. Ren believes that she mainly takes after her own mother, given her own unwavering commitment towards making Yuigaoka the number one school in her city. While she was a bit too serious at first, Ren mellows out since joining Liella! and shows her true personality; she is certainly responsible and diligent, but also friendly, kind, and good-natured, also showing quite a playful streak. She also seems to have quite the naive side to her, shown through her curiosity and innocence of various things. She is easily slightly embarrassed about these traits, her formal tone sometimes falling apart. Overall, she is still very responsible, reliant, resilient, and one for order, and is accepted by the members of Liella! and those who respect her. Her favorite saying is, 'Never forget why you started.'

name: Kinako Sakurakoji
personality: Kinako is not very athletic, and her grades are average. However, Kinako is a hard worker who does not back down easily from challenges. She is very good at understanding animals and how they are feeling. She is very kind, sweet and cheerful, and always happy to offer her support to the group. Despite being sociable, she is also a little shy and awkward at times. She has a tendency to be very loud and can be easily startled. She is somewhat eccentric and comes across as a bit of an airhead. However, she is shown to be level-headed at the end of the day. Her assessments can come off as innocently insensitive since she is honest to a fault.

name: Mei Yoneme
personality: Mei speaks to strangers in a rather abrasive tone and comes off as frightening to other students, but she is actually very shy and has a diehard adoration for school idols that she keeps secret out of fear of being judged. Mei gets embarrassed when she is called 'cute'. After joining, Mei starts to come out of her shell, and becomes much more comfortable being herself around her new friends. Her tone of speaking changes from sarcastic and dry to calm and soft during live performances. She is very close with Shiki, and confides in her regularly about her troubles.

name: Shiki Wakana
personality: Shiki is a stoic girl who has difficulty expressing herself. She is not very fond of loud places. Her favorite things are Mei, stag beetles and butterflies. She appears to have a calm demeanor and is quiet most of the time. She also does not smile much, but the reason why is yet to be determined. She is also very conscious of her surroundings, as she easily noticed Mei looking at her from the Science Club room while she is with the other Liella! members at the rooftop. At her core, Shiki is a kind and well meaning girl who does her best to support her best friend; initially becoming a member of the school idol club on a trial period in an attempt to bait Mei into joining. Eventually, she officially joins the group in order to spend more time with Mei and bond over their newly found shared interest. However, despite her gentle nature, Shiki can become noticeably jealous, clingy and possessive when Mei spends time with others, as well as remarking about Mei's looks almost every chance she gets. She has a strange unexplained habit of blurting out English sentences at random occasions. Shiki prefers to stay in the shade, as the light is too bright for her. She has a habit of fainting when placed in situations that are too much for her.

name: Natsumi Onitsuka
personality: Natsumi has a friendly demeanor, but can also be rather manipulative and selfish; she initially gets involved with Liella! to take advantage of their growing popularity to obtain money and internet fame through ad revenue as a social media influencer. Somewhat similarly to others, Natsumi also spent her childhood trying to break into different fields of entertainment before eventually giving up, albeit with the sole intention of making money. After spending enough time with the group and enjoying time with them, she took a genuine liking to the charm of school idols. When it comes to her sister Tomari, Natsumi constantly praises her when she gets the chance to. Natsumi frequently ends her sentences with '-desu no'. Her catchphrase is 'OniNatsui'.

name: Wien Margarete
personality: Margarete is a cold-blooded, passionate and stoic girl, who can act recklessly or overdramatically when fussy. She is extremely competitive and outright hostile to other idols she sees as threats. Originally signing up as a school idol contestant as a solo idol, she boldly makes it known that she has no interest in either school idols or other competitions other than setting out to prove she can easily win the competition by herself. Once forming a unit with Kanon, Margarete slowly reveals her kinder side, but she never loses her competitive nature. Margarete is not fond of people who do not put in a lot of effort. Her favorite saying is, 'Stick to your guns.'

name: Tomari Onitsuka
personality: Tomari dislikes wasting time. Tomari is organized, analytical but kind, even though her words can be misinterpreted. Her favorite saying is, 'Dreams cannot nourish you.' Tomari views school idol activities as a potential income stream that will generate profit for her family. However, Tomari's personality comes off as stiff and calculating to others. Because of her natural talent for dancing, she does not see much of a point in showing up to practice sessions unless she has something to gain from attending. While she can be very aloof, Tomari has a deep respect for her older sister, and decides to follow in her sister's example after seeing how much being a school idol meant to her.

name: Ginko Momose
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Ginko has always had an interest in school idols, owing to her grandmother being an alumna of the Hasunosora Performing Arts Club. She dreams of becoming a school idol as a way of inheriting this tradition from her grandmother. Ginko practices her family's trade of Kaga embroidery, and loves to design and make new clothing, which she hopes to be able to do for the School Idol Club. She particularly likes Japanese clothing and is also skilled in traditional Japanese dance. Ginko likes taking long walks to admire historical buildings, such as Shinto shrines and Buddhist temples, and also frequents coffee shops. Ginko's weakness is always trying to go with the flow so she's not viewed as weird, which also makes her come across as stubborn. Ginko needs time to prepare because she's afraid of failure. Ginko is afraid of admitting of how she really feels, because she doesn't want to annoy anyone. Ginko comes across as more tsundere-ish towards Kaho, though she has trouble taking compliments in general and reacts with the typical denials.

name: Kosuzu Kachimachi
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Kosuzu first appears as a frazzled, panicked girl who believes she has never achieved anything. She thinks lowly of herself and has a habit of putting herself down, stating things like, 'It'd be better if I wasn't around'. She constantly worries if she's bothering others, calling herself a shameless good for nothing that 'has only ever been a burden to others'. It is for this reason that she makes up seemingly pointless goals, such as crossing the lake, in order to feel like she's accomplished something. But despite the many goals she makes for herself, she doesn't believe that any will come true, stating that 'there isn't the tiniest fraction of a chance that everything will go perfectly'. But as she gets to know Tsuzuri and Sayaka, she is instilled with a new drive: to finally become something as a member of DOLLCHESTRA. Sayaka, recognizing her will power as being similar to her own, respects her relentless motivation and hard work, likening her to a hard-working, bright yellow color. Kosuzu's biggest weakness is giving up before she's even started something, and is oblivious to the fact she doesn't get the difference between courage and recklessness. However, her biggest strength is that since she spends so much time watching and being envious of others, she's good at identifying people's strengths. She's also good at complimenting people and making them feel more confident about themselves.

name: Hime Anyoji
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Hime is extremely passionate about gaming and school idols, especially towards Mira-Cra Park!, whom she adores. She only ever thinks about these two things, often using gaming references in regular conversation. Hime has a tendency to yell 'UOOOOOH!' whenever she gets hyped up, but she doesn't seem to notice it. The same can be said for her speech: it's usually slower in tempo, but gets much faster when fangirl mode. She is much more laid-back and easygoing when talking to classmates and club members outside of her unit. She loves battles, whether in games or real life, because they show your prowess and how much practice you've put in. She likes that it shows one's mental fortitude, and says that she truly feels alive when competing. There's no other place she can feel such thrill and restlessness. But her true passion in life is connecting with others, or as she calls it, a 'multiplayer match'. In games, this means that she can play with others despite being alone, and in the school idol club this refers to her being able to spend her days training and playing with her friends. What she loves the most is the feeling of connecting with those around her. Despite being aware of everyone's weaknesses, Hime chooses not to say anything because she was afraid talking about ways on how to improve would make them quit. This made Hime worry about whether she was taking things too seriously.

name: Izumi Katsuragi
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Izumi is a skilled dancer, who is much younger than her looks and maturity would make her seem. Although this is partly due to her referring to herself as 'onee-san' even among girls who are the same age. She's very perceptive when it comes to performances and can instantly tell where someone is lacking. She is a tenacious girl with a lot of self-confidence, and takes her duty to win very seriously, even going so far as to say that her entire reason for living is to make Ceras's dream come true. She says this is because she loves people who chase their dreams. Izumi can handle most things with ease. Despite only just joining the school idol club she is extremely talented at what she does, tearing through the competition despite being the youngest one there. However, she doesn't actually take any pleasure in winning herself.

name: Ceras Yanagida Lilienfeld
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Ceras is a soft-spoken but determined girl who dreams of being a school idol. Her love for school idols leads her to always thinking about them during class. She is carefree and likes to joke around with her childhood friend, Kaho. The two are still very close, with Ceras calling her 'Hana-chan' and Kaho calling her 'Sechan'. Despite Kaho being her rival in the competition, she remains friendly towards her, inviting her over to her school to tell her all about her goals. She's glad when Kaho succeeds but still wishes to come out on top in the competition, letting the two have a healthy rivalry. She is an extremely hard worker, going above and beyond to ensure that her dream comes true. Due to her time as an official club member, she manages others well and is very capable for her age. Kaho says that she exaggerates a lot to try and get people's attention. She also has quite a teasing side, enjoying getting people like Ginko riled up.

name: Maika Kinjo
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Maika likes to express her thoughts directly, and is a stubborn person who doesn't easily change her mind. However, as soon as she feels that she has said too much, she immediately reflects on it. Although her way of doing things can be very clumsy, she has a kind heart and is always willing to reach out to those in need.

name: Aoi Reisawa
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Aoi is good at getting others to spoil her, and she is skilled at asserting herself, even towards her seniors. Though she may seem selfish at first glance, she can also be very considerate, like when she supports Maika.

name: Sachi Ogami
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Sachi is a strong-spoken girl who stands up for what she believes in. Upon seeing that the board was trying to implement harsh rules for the student body that would make life at the school a lot less fun, she stepped up to become the Student Council President in order to fight back and try to ease those rules. She is especially protective of the School Idol Club, and will always make the hard but right decision over the easy but wrong decision. She is hard working and somewhat mischievous, and enjoys teasing Kaho Hinoshita and Rurino Osawa. She considers herself a mentor for the School Idol Club and still considers herself to be a school idol even though she had to quit the club. Sachi hates comments about her height but enjoys acting as a mentor to those in the school. She would rather guide people towards answers rather than outright tell them what to do, and considers herself a meddler in a good way.

name: Kozue Otomune
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: A handsome honor student who is good at both academics and sports. Kozue is graceful and elegant. Even though she is respected and admired by everyone at Hasunosora, she is never boastful nor complacent. She spares no effort in anything she does. She is bad with technology, but she insists that she's on the way to making it her strong point. Kozue tends to be a perfectionist, but only when it comes to herself: she has extremely high standards for herself and tends to blame herself when things go wrong. However, she is reasonable enough to listen to others when necessary. Kozue told Kaho Hinoshita that she used to be much worse about this, and that she used to set such impossibly high standards for herself when it came to music that she had little time for anything else. Kozue is very formal and proper in behavior. She speaks and addresses everyone around her formally, and only drops honorifics for Tsuzuri Yugiri, Megumi Fujishima, and later Kaho Hinoshita. Tsuzuri notes that Kozue and Megumi used to have frequent arguments and shouting matches, indicating that Kozue has a temper beneath her polished exterior. Her hobbies are stargazing, music appreciation, and making teacakes. Her special skills are classical ballet and strength training. She can play the piano, the violin, the trumpet, the flute, the saxophone, and the guitar. She also composes her own songs, and prefers to do so either on the piano or with her guitar. However, she is not a good artist. Her favorite foods are black tea and boiled tofu, and she enjoys art class, world history class, and cats. Her favorite phrase is 'Stand like a peony, sit like a peony, walk like a lily', and it is used to describe the demeanor of a beautiful woman. The slender stems of the peony represent their slim body when standing, the way the peony blooms on sideways branches gives them the appearance of someone sitting on them, and lilies sway elegantly in the wind like a woman walking.

name: Tsuzuri Yugiri
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: She is absent-minded, always in a daze, and talks with a peculiar flair, but an excellent performer when on stage. Because of this, people often call her a genius and consider her difficult to approach. Fans even swarm her performance in the hallways when she has them. However, in reality, she is easily lonely and very friendly, resembling a rather clingy child. Because of this demeanor, the rest of the club has become her caretakers, and she loves them endlessly in return. She has a very difficult time explaining herself, and this affects both her teaching and social skills. Her dancing advice is incomprehensible to anyone but Sayaka Murano, and she has immense trouble conveying her inner thoughts and problems. However, despite her oddities, she is an extremely kind and sensitive person. She excels at listening to others and giving them support, and is able to be a calming presence to those who are troubled. She says that this is fun for her, and that she likes to see people smile after she alleviates their worries. Regarding Sayaka, she has a great attachment towards her, and the two are often seen together. In WithxMeets on 4/22/23, she outright states that, 'My favorite is Saya, and Saya's favorite is me'. She has the responsibility of awakening Tsuzuri in the mornings, making her lunch, guiding her around, and other tasks. After she made up with Kozue, she is also quite affectionate towards her, as even though Kozue can be exasperated with her. She has the habit of shortening people's names to just two characters, calling Sayaka 'Saya', Kozue 'Kozu', Megumi 'Megu', Rurino 'Ruri', and Ginko 'Gin'. Three syllables is too long for her. Her hobbies are watching anything that moves and listening to others talk. She sometimes squats on the ground to observe things that move, such as ants during her introduction. Her special skill is that she's proud of how fast she can fall asleep. Her favorite food is chocolate cake, and she likes 'warm creatures' and mathematics. 'Amicable harmony' is her favorite phrase.

name: Megumi Fujishima
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: She is self-conscious of her own cuteness, so she spares no effort in order to be seen as cute. This can be seen in her creation of 'ハロ◇く/Hello Megu', the unique greeting she's made for herself, along with it's variants (such as 'バイ◇め</Bye Megu'). During this greeting, she sticks her right arm out and winks her right eye, making it her signature pose. Her academic performance is poor since she absolutely won't do things she's not interested in, and she doesn't even know that the word 'proofs' relates to math. She spends almost every day in supplementary classes due to failing almost every test. In regards to her favorite subject, she simply says 'It's okay to not study because it won't help you in the future'. She and Rurino Osawa are friends since their middle school. Being a year older, she strives to be ahead of Rurino, wanting to appear as the cool, older girl. However, despite her gravitas, she holds fear and insecurity deep in her heart. She can become quite upset when losing, with Rurino needing to calm her down lest she spiral into depression or further anger. Her hobbies are streaming and editing work (which she considers 'plain, but important'). Her special skills are drawing girls and making accessories, and she enjoys monaka (azuki bean paste sandwiched between thin wafers made from mochi) and negitoro (a type of sushi). Her favorite animal is a German Shepherd, and her favorite quote is 'Love is what makes you good at things'.

name: Kaho Hinoshita
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: An energetic girl who has a smile on her face no matter what. She entered the academy with high expectations and the goal of 'blossoming', as her parents heavily restricted her freedom beforehand. Blossoming, according to her, is how others looked while freely enjoying their daily lives. She thought this way of living was beautiful, comparing it to flowers you can't look away from. She thinks that by attaining freedom, she can blossom too. Kaho's greatest fear is being left alone, likely stemming from her lonely childhood. This loneliness combines with her eager attitude, making her a tad too friendly towards strangers (such as Sayaka), and she noted as a talkative person. Fortunately, this cheerful personality also allows her to make friends quite easily. She approached Sayaka Murano on the train just because of their shared uniform, despite never meeting her before, instantly sparking a conversation. Sayaka was uncomfortable at first, forced to listen to her eager ramblings, but the two were still able to become friends rather quickly. She initially hated the academy upon realizing its strict rules, including an exact curfew, discipline, and needing written permission to leave school grounds. This culminated in a plan to escape, but after the coaxing of Kozue Otomune, she realized school idols could help her enjoy it in her own way. She ended up wanting to make Hasunosora more fun for the students, despite not wanting to take part in the club originally. She is a kind-hearted, friendly, cheerful, passionate, welcoming, and supportive person who strives to make everything fun for everyone. She puts all her energy into what she enjoys, although can become somewhat impulsive and charge ahead in the wrong direction. Her hobbies are karaoke and reading manga, and her special skill is that she isn't a picky eater. She enjoys apples, Hanton rice (a local dish of Kanazawa City), physical education class, and music class. She loves all animals, but rabbits in particular are her favorite. Her favorite pose is making a V-sign with her hand while winking. Her favorite quote is 'Do your best in everything!'

name: Sayaka Murano
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: Sayaka is awkward, but also a genuine hard worker who doesn't give up when times are tough. Even though her grades are good, but she has trouble when it comes to her personal life. This is because she can be too serious, even giving simple lunchboxes her all. She also can't bring herself to neglect other people, and can unintentionally give help where it isn't wanted. Upon entering Hasunosora, she initially had no desire for friends, likely because of her lack of humor and sometimes clumsy social skills. Sayaka is very formal and always makes sure to address others formally. She is more reserved and stiff than her fellow 1st years, Kaho Hinoshita and Rurino Osawa, and often worries that her stiffness might drive others away. However, these mannerisms prove invaluable for her relationship with Tsuzuri: by listening patiently and nudging her along with questions, Sayaka can help her sort out complicated situations. This dynamic between Sayaka's grounded nature and Tsuzuri's light and airy personality allows for a successful partnership. She is unable to leave others alone when they need help, as is shown in her relationship with Tsuzuri. Sayaka wakes her in the mornings, makes her lunch, takes her to practice, and generally keeps her on track. Kozue Otomune expressed pity towards taking care of her, but Sayaka doesn't mind the added responsibility, even finding new joy in cooking after making Tsuzuri's bentos. She can talk at length about Tsuzuri's performances, and greatly respects (and envies) her inherent talent. This is expanded upon with the arrival of Kosuzu, as she is now her senpai and mentor. She feels a great deal of responsibility towards her, and will go to great lengths to help her. Her hobbies are making boxed lunches and researching boxed lunches (both store-bought and station-bought varieties). Her special skill is planning things perfectly, and her favorite phrase means 'A strong will brings success'. Her favorite subjects include modern literature and mathematics, and her favorite animal is a red panda.

name: Rurino Osawa
group: Hasunosora Girls' High School Idol Club
personality: She is a cheerful and energetic girl, able to find enjoyment in anything and anyone. However, she must always consider her 'battery', a sort of social battery that depletes quickly. If this gets too low, then she loses all her energy and ability to think, being unable to communicate properly and appearing extremely lethargic and sluggish. In order to preserve this energy, she has to quickly find time to herself, treating it as a recharging period. Because of her energy's short limit, she enjoys solitary activities and places in nature. Because of her time in California, she claims that she can speak English. However, in fact, her English is not very good, and is something that only Kaho is impressed by. Her hobbies are fishing, karaoke, and going to the batting cages. Her special skill is 'finding fun in anything!', and she enjoys art and music classes. Her favorite foods are tapioca and kohakuto (meaning 'amber sugar', a traditional Japanese candy), her favorite animals are chow chows and big bears, and her favorite phrase is 'The heart that does not want to enter that path is your own personal master'. This saying is included in a traditional tea ceremony guide, and it means that it's difficult to continue something unejoyable just because others tell you to. In order to venture upon a path, you must want to do it and take an initiative in it, and this mindset becomes one's master in this analogy. Since she spends much of her energy trying to make others happy, she pays the most attention to how others are feeling. She's very perceptive about this and will notice right away if someone is feeling down.
